highest_risk_seam: T027→T028→T029 (clearing engine → SPAN margin → daily settlement)

tasks.title: Cloud Architecture Design
tasks.description: Design AWS dual-region active/passive architecture. Primary: ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo), DR: ap-southeast-1 (Singapore). EKS + Istio, RDS PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB, Redis, Kafka. ADR document + Terraform variable definitions.

tasks.title: Terraform IaC Modules
tasks.description: Create Terraform modules for VPC (3-tier subnets), EKS (5 node groups), RDS (PostgreSQL 15), MSK (Kafka 3.5), security groups. S3+DynamoDB state backend. Workspaces: dev/staging/prod.

tasks.title: EKS Cluster + Istio Service Mesh
tasks.description: Deploy EKS cluster per ADR-001 spec. Install Istio with mTLS enforcement. Configure Karpenter autoscaler, NodeLocal DNSCache, VPC CNI plugin. Validate 5 node groups come up healthy.
tasks.notes: Originally rejected, reworked via T031 which was merged to main

tasks.title: Core Database Schema
tasks.description: Create Flyway migrations V1–V5: reference, participants, exchange, clearing, compliance, warehouse, auth, market_data schemas. Append-only trades table, SHA-256 audit hash chain, TimescaleDB hypertables. Seed 14 commodities, 7 delivery locations, 2025–2026 trading calendar.

tasks.title: Auth & IAM Service
tasks.description: Build authentication/authorization microservice. JWT + OAuth2 PKCE + RBAC. Integrate with IRSA for AWS service access. User/API-key/session management against auth schema from T004.
tasks.notes: Status fixed: review APPROVED, was incorrectly marked rejected

tasks.title: CI/CD Pipeline
tasks.description: Set up GitHub Actions workflows: build, test, lint, security scan, container image push, Terraform plan/apply. Branch protection rules. Deployment to dev/staging/prod EKS clusters.

tasks.title: Exchange Engine Architecture Spec
tasks.description: Design the CLOB (Central Limit Order Book) architecture, order matching algorithms (price-time priority), price discovery mechanism, trade ledger design. Produce spec doc + API contracts for builder agents.

tasks.title: Order Matching Engine
tasks.description: Implement the core matching engine in Go. Price-time priority CLOB. Sub-50ms latency target at 10,000 orders/sec. Direct pod-to-pod comms (bypass Istio sidecar). Append-only trade writes.

tasks.title: KYC/AML Architecture Spec
tasks.description: Design KYC/AML workflows: participant onboarding, document verification, watchlist screening, risk scoring. API contracts for builder agents.

tasks.title: Clearing Engine
tasks.description: Build clearing engine for trade novation, position management, and netting. Part of highest-risk seam (T027→T028→T029).

tasks.title: SPAN Margin Calculation
tasks.description: Implement SPAN-style margin model for commodity futures. Real-time margin calculation, margin call generation.

tasks.title: Daily Settlement
tasks.description: Implement daily mark-to-market settlement cycle. Position valuation, P&L calculation, settlement instruction generation, payment gateway integration.

tasks.title: Rework EKS+Istio — Fix 3 Reviewer Bugs
tasks.description:
Rework T003 (EKS Cluster + Istio Service Mesh) fixing the 3 bugs identified in handoff/T003-review.md:

1. **Duplicate YAML key in `deploy/helm/istio/values-istiod.yaml`**: `meshConfig.defaultConfig` appears twice. Merge into one block so `holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true` is preserved alongside `tracing` config.
2. **DestinationRule host mismatch in `deploy/k8s/base/istio-mtls.yaml`**: Change `host: "*.local"` to `host: "*.svc.cluster.local"` for correct in-mesh service matching.
3. **`node_groups` variable type in `variables.tf`**: Add optional `labels` and `taints` fields to match actual usage in `local.default_node_groups`.

Also add a YAML lint validation step to the test suite per reviewer suggestion. Read handoff/T003-review.md for full context. Branch from existing feature/T003-eks-istio work.

tasks.title: Compliance Service (KYC/AML)
tasks.description:
Implement compliance-service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/compliance-service/`. Follow the T015 KYC/AML spec (read handoff/T015-review.md and the spec artifacts). Ports: gRPC 50056, health HTTP 8086.

Features from T015 spec:
- Participant onboarding workflow (PENDING → DOCUMENTS_SUBMITTED → UNDER_REVIEW → APPROVED/REJECTED)
- Document verification service (ID, proof of address, business registration)
- Watchlist screening (sanctions, PEP, adverse media) with configurable providers
- Risk scoring engine (low/medium/high/critical tiers)
- Ongoing monitoring and periodic re-screening
- gRPC API: CreateApplication, SubmitDocuments, RunScreening, GetRiskScore, ApproveParticipant
- SQL migrations for compliance schema (participants, documents, screenings, risk_scores)

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern (copy Decimal type if needed). Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests targeting 60%+ coverage on business logic. Write handoff/T032.md on completion.

tasks.title: API Gateway Architecture Spec
tasks.description:
Design the API gateway service that provides REST→gRPC translation for all exchange engines. Produce spec doc + API contracts.

Scope:
- REST API design for all existing gRPC services: matching-engine (50051), clearing-engine (50052), margin-engine (50053), settlement-engine (50054), auth-service (50055), compliance-service (50056)
- HTTP port 8080 for all REST endpoints
- Authentication middleware (JWT validation via auth-service)
- Rate limiting design (per-user, per-endpoint)
- Request/response mapping (REST JSON ↔ gRPC protobuf)
- WebSocket support for real-time order book and trade streams
- API versioning strategy (v1 prefix)
- OpenAPI 3.0 spec for all endpoints
- Error code mapping (gRPC status codes → HTTP status codes)

Output: architecture doc at `docs/T033_gateway_spec.md`, OpenAPI skeleton, endpoint mapping table. Write handoff/T033.md on completion.

tasks.title: API Gateway Service
tasks.description:
Implement the API gateway as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/gateway/`. Port: HTTP 8080. Read handoff/T033.md for the spec.

Features:
- REST→gRPC translation layer for all exchange services
- JWT authentication middleware (validate tokens, extract claims, enforce RBAC)
- Endpoint routing: POST /v1/orders → matching-engine, GET /v1/positions → clearing-engine, GET /v1/margin → margin-engine, POST /v1/settlement → settlement-engine, POST /v1/auth/* → auth-service, POST /v1/compliance/* → compliance-service
- Request validation and JSON→protobuf marshaling
- gRPC client connection pool management
- Rate limiting (token bucket per API key)
- WebSocket upgrade for /v1/ws/orderbook and /v1/ws/trades
- Health check at /healthz
- Structured JSON logging
- Graceful shutdown

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests for routing, auth middleware, and request translation. Write handoff/T034.md on completion.

tasks.title: Market Data Service Architecture Spec
tasks.description:
Design the market-data-service for OHLCV aggregation from trade streams. Produce spec doc + API contracts.

Scope:
- OHLCV (Open/High/Low/Close/Volume) candle aggregation from matching-engine trade stream
- Time intervals: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d
- TimescaleDB continuous aggregates for efficient historical queries
- Real-time streaming via gRPC server-side streaming and WebSocket
- Ticker/summary endpoint (last price, 24h change, 24h volume, bid/ask)
- Trade tape (recent trades list)
- gRPC API: GetCandles, StreamCandles, GetTicker, GetTrades, StreamTrades
- SQL migrations for market_data schema (trades hypertable, OHLCV continuous aggregates)
- Data retention policy (tick data 90 days, 1m candles 1 year, daily candles indefinite)

Ports: gRPC 50057, health HTTP 8087. Output: spec doc at `docs/T035_market_data_spec.md`. Write handoff/T035.md on completion.

tasks.title: Market Data Service
tasks.description:
Implement market-data-service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/market-data-service/`. Ports: gRPC 50057, health HTTP 8087. Read handoff/T035.md for the spec.

Features:
- Trade ingestion from matching-engine (gRPC client or in-process channel)
- OHLCV candle aggregation engine (1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d intervals)
- TimescaleDB continuous aggregate DDL generation
- In-memory candle builder with periodic flush to DB
- gRPC server-side streaming for real-time candle and trade updates
- Historical candle query with time range and interval selection
- Ticker/summary computation (last, 24h high/low, 24h volume, 24h change %)
- Trade tape (last N trades per instrument)
- Data retention policy enforcement
- Health check endpoint

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests for candle aggregation logic (60%+ coverage on business logic). Write handoff/T036.md on completion.

tasks.title: Warehouse Service Architecture Spec
tasks.description:
Design the warehouse-service for physical commodity receipt management. Produce spec doc + API contracts.

Scope:
- Electronic Warehouse Receipt (eWR) lifecycle: issuance, transfer, cancellation, delivery
- Warehouse/storage facility registry with capacity tracking
- Commodity inventory management (quantity, grade, location, lot tracking)
- Delivery instruction processing (delivery vs cash settlement)
- Quality inspection and grading workflow
- Receipt collateralization (pledge/release for margin)
- gRPC API: IssueReceipt, TransferReceipt, CancelReceipt, InitiateDelivery, CompleteDelivery, GetInventory, PledgeReceipt, ReleaseReceipt
- SQL migrations for warehouse schema (receipts, facilities, inventory, deliveries, inspections)
- Integration points with clearing-engine (delivery obligations) and margin-engine (collateral)

Ports: gRPC 50058, health HTTP 8088. Output: spec doc at `docs/T037_warehouse_spec.md`. Write handoff/T037.md on completion.

tasks.title: Warehouse Service
tasks.description:
Implement warehouse-service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/warehouse-service/`. Ports: gRPC 50058, health HTTP 8088. Read handoff/T037.md for the spec.

Features:
- eWR (Electronic Warehouse Receipt) management: issue, transfer, cancel with full audit trail
- Warehouse facility registry (name, location, capacity, commodity types accepted)
- Commodity inventory tracking (quantity, grade, lot number, storage location)
- Delivery workflow: initiate → inspect → approve → complete/reject
- Quality grading system (grade definitions per commodity from T004 seed data)
- Receipt collateralization: pledge receipt as margin collateral, release on margin call resolution
- Receipt uniqueness enforcement (one active receipt per lot)
- gRPC service implementation for all API endpoints
- SQL repository layer with parameterized queries
- Health check endpoint

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests for receipt lifecycle and delivery workflow (60%+ coverage on business logic). Write handoff/T038.md on completion.

tasks.title: Phase 3 Integration Test
tasks.description:
Run full integration test across all services built in Phase 3. Verify:
- All Go modules compile: compliance-service, gateway, market-data-service, warehouse-service
- All unit tests pass across all services (including Phase 1-2 services: matching-engine, clearing-engine, margin-engine, settlement-engine, auth-service)
- Coverage meets floors: 60% for business-critical packages, 30% for infrastructure
- No import cycles or dependency conflicts between modules
- All Dockerfiles build successfully
- EKS+Istio rework passes structural tests

Report SKIP if no compilable code found (per learned pattern). Write handoff/integration-run-<timestamp>.md.

tasks.title: Rebuild Auth Service
tasks.description:
Rebuild the auth service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/auth-service/`. The previous T005 implementation was approved but never merged to main — src/auth-service/ currently contains only a stub Dockerfile. Read handoff/T005-review.md for the approved design.

Ports: gRPC 50055, health HTTP 8085.

Features (from T005 review):
- JWT access/refresh tokens (HMAC signing, `AUTH_JWT_SIGNING_KEY` from env)
- OAuth2 PKCE flow (S256 only — do NOT support plain method per reviewer suggestion)
- RBAC with roles and permissions (super_admin, admin, trader, viewer, compliance_officer)
- User registration with bcrypt password hashing (cost 12)
- Login with account lockout (5 failed attempts, 30-minute duration)
- API key management (create, validate, revoke) — keys stored as SHA-256 hashes
- Session management (create, refresh, revoke)
- Refresh token rotation (address the gap noted in T005 review: ExchangeCode must store refresh token hash on session)
- SQL repository layer with parameterized queries (never string concatenation)
- DB SSL mode defaults to `require`
- HTTP handlers with input validation (required fields, minimum password length, email format)
- Health check endpoint at /healthz
- Graceful shutdown with signal handling
- Dockerfile running as non-root user (appuser, UID 1000)

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests targeting 60%+ coverage on business logic (JWT, auth service, PKCE, handlers). Write handoff/T040.md on completion.
tasks.notes: Merge failed due to untracked Dockerfile on main — manually resolved and merged

tasks.title: Fix PostMortem Context Size Bug
tasks.description:
Fix the `build_postmortem_context` function in `pipeline/lib/context.sh` that concatenates ALL handoff files into a single shell variable, which is then passed as a `-p` argument to `claude`. By Phase 3, this exceeded the Linux ARG_MAX limit (~2MB), causing `Argument list too long` (E2BIG).

Fix approach: write the assembled postmortem prompt to a temp file, then pipe it via stdin to `claude` instead of passing as `-p` argument. Specifically:
1. In `build_postmortem_context`, keep the prompt assembly logic as-is (it builds the correct content).
2. In the caller (likely `pipeline/run.sh` or wherever `build_postmortem_context` is invoked), change from `claude -p "$(build_postmortem_context)"` to writing the output to a temp file and using stdin: `build_postmortem_context > "$tmpfile" && claude < "$tmpfile"`.
3. Also consider only passing handoff files from the current run (filter by task IDs in tasks.json with status changes in this run) rather than ALL historical handoff files, to keep context focused.
4. Clean up temp file on exit (trap).

Test by verifying the postmortem prompt can be generated for the current project state (20+ handoff files) without hitting ARG_MAX. Write handoff/T041.md on completion.
tasks.notes: Merge failed due to untracked pipeline/ files on main — manually resolved and merged

tasks.title: Clean Up Service Naming Stubs
tasks.description:
Remove stub Dockerfile-only directories that conflict with actual service locations:

1. Remove `src/clearing-service/` entirely (real code is at `src/clearing-engine/`). This directory only has a stub Dockerfile.
2. Verify no other stub directories conflict: check each `src/*/` directory — if it contains only a Dockerfile and no Go source code, and a corresponding `-engine` or differently-named directory has the real code, remove the stub.
3. Update any references to removed directories in docker-compose.yml, Makefile, CI configs (.github/workflows/), or Dockerfiles.
4. Do NOT rename any directories that contain real code.

Expected removals: `src/clearing-service/` (stub, real code at `src/clearing-engine/`). Verify `src/auth-service/` is kept (T040 will rebuild it with real code).

Write handoff/T042.md on completion.

tasks.title: Clearing Engine Test Coverage Improvement
tasks.description:
Improve test coverage on business-critical packages in `src/clearing-engine/`. Current coverage is far below the 60% floor:
- `internal/novation/` — 23.7% → target 60%+
- `internal/netting/` — 39.8% → target 60%+
- `internal/engine/` — 32.8% → target 60%+

Read the existing code and tests first. Add tests for uncovered paths:
- **novation**: test multilateral novation, partial novation failures, CCP interposition edge cases, novation with different position types
- **netting**: test bilateral netting, multilateral netting, netting with mixed buy/sell positions, netting calculation edge cases (zero positions, single position)
- **engine**: test trade processing pipeline end-to-end, position updates after clearing, error handling for invalid trades, concurrent trade processing

Do NOT modify existing production code — only add test files. Follow existing test patterns in the package. Use table-driven tests where appropriate. Target 60%+ coverage on each package, 80%+ on novation (financial-critical).

Write handoff/T043.md on completion with per-package coverage numbers.
